threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of just 'the train.' Once you blend in, it'll just be 'the DLR' for you, too.
Caught the N59 last night; the driver sang along to every tune on the radio. Brixton's nightlife never sleeps, not even on the buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I truly belong when I saw a whole group of kids playing soccer in the middle of the street while a bus honked but just waited patiently.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand with vegan options open till 4 AM. That’s the real Berlin night life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you don’t flinch at the sound of a loud bang from BART—you’ve learned it’s just Tuesday in this city.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy with a pizza box waving Muni down like a taxi at Civic Center. Only in SF do we turn delays into dinner plans.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think 'siesta' means shops close all afternoon. Nah, we’re just waiting for the heat to pass before heading out after 6!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with their T-10 cards at the metro machines, staring blankly when the screen glitches.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it ‘the Tube’ instead of just ‘transport’ like us locals. You’ll get there, just gotta ditch the tourist lingo!
Saw a bloke on the 35 last night openly reading a script for a play. He looked so serious, but no one else seemed to care. Just another Brixton night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a family hosting a barbecue in the middle of a street party while a bus somehow squeezed through—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a massive mural of a cat wearing sunglasses next to a vegan café named after a famous philosopher. Pure Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local when they know the ‘secret’ taco truck by the empty lot on 24th—struction workers swear by their salsa.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of a dog lounging on the tracks at Civic Center. Muni's delays are a staple, but a pooch casually blocking the train? Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all trendy bars; they miss the quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet and gossip in Catalan.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they stare blankly at the metro map, still clutching it like a lifeline—just ask any old-timer.
